prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
Chinese: "The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now." - Living in the world of practical and short-sighted decisions, Wise saying states that the perfect time to plant a tree was in the past, and the next best time is now. This reflects the importance of seizing opportunities that are right in front of us.

German: "The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now." - This encourages immediate action, not procrastinating and dreaming about perfect opportunities, and instead taking advantages that are present in the moment.

English: "The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now." - Conveying the idea that while the ideal time for planting a tree has passed, the current time is still excellent for doing so. The proverb advises against procrastination and encourages seizing opportunities that are present.

French: "The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now." - Reminding people that while perfect opportunities might have existed in the past, today provides another excellent chance to take action, showing that it is always better to start working on something than putting it off for a later time.

Spanish: "The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now." - Besides emphasizing the importance of taking action in the present, the saying also suggests that while the perfect time to plant a tree might have passed, the second best time is now. This reflects the idea that while some actions might have a perfect time in the past, there is always another adequate time to start in the present.

Russian: "The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now." - Highlighting the importance of not delaying important tasks, the Russian version of this proverb emphasizes that while the best time for planting a tree might have been in the past, now is also an excellent time to do so. This reflects the value of seizing every opportunity that is currently available. #### 5
